title: Configure the Script Editor
text: Configure the Script Editor The Script Editor enables you to write ProcScript and JavaScript that controls the behavior of the component. Uniface also allows you to configure the Script Editor settings. Related Links Configure Indentation in the Script Editor By default, the Script Editor is configured to indent code using a tab character that is equivalent to 4 spaces. If your coding standards or preferences call for the use of spaces or a different tab size, you can change this configuration. Enabling bi-directional writing for an RTL language Right-to-left (RTL) languages are written from right to left of the screen, such as Arabic, Hebrew, and Persian languages. English is a Left-to-right (LTR) language. Bi-directional writing supports both RTL and LTR languages simultaneously. By default, bi-directional writing is disabled in Uniface IDE editor. To enable bi-directional writing for an RTL language, you need to update the usys.ini file. Configuring the auto-indentation feature Auto-indent is an automated feature in the Uniface Script Editor that automatically adjusts the indentation of Proc lines, ensuring consistent and readable formatting. This feature is enabled by default and can be disabled by setting autoindent=off in the usys.ini file. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
